title: Tel Aviv 10-Day Beach Getaway
metaTitle: Tel Aviv 10-Day Beach Getaway | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 10-day trip to Tel Aviv, Israel: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Tel Aviv Museum of Art and ANU Museum of the Jewish People. Prices from $2313 per person (estimate).
highlights: ANU Museum of the Jewish People
overview: Spend 10 days discovering Tel Aviv, Israel — Mediterranean climate — hot, humid summers (highs around 30-31°C) and mild, occasionally rainy winters (highs around 18°C). April-May and September-October, for warm, comfortable weather without peak summer humidity. This itinerary blends Tel Aviv Museum of Art, ANU Museum of the Jewish People, Tel Aviv beaches with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
9 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Tel Aviv Museum of Art, ANU Museum of the Jewish People, Tel Aviv beaches
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Tel Aviv
itinerary.description: Land at Ben Gurion Airport (TLV) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Tel Aviv Museum of Art — Major collection of modern and contemporary art. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Tel Aviv: ANU Museum of the Jewish People & Old Jaffa walking tour
itinerary.description: Morning visit to ANU Museum of the Jewish People — Comprehensive museum on Jewish history and culture. In the afternoon, continue to Old Jaffa walking tour — Guided tour of the ancient port town's alleys and flea market. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: ANU Museum of the Jewish People

itinerary.title: Tel Aviv: Independence Hall & Tel Aviv beaches
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Independence Hall — Site where Israel's independence was declared in 1948. In the afternoon, continue to Tel Aviv beaches — A long stretch of free, lively urban beaches along the Mediterranean. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Jerusalem (45 minutes)
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Jerusalem (45 minutes), a popular side trip from Tel Aviv. Return to Tel Aviv in the evening with time free to explore on your own.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Levinsky Market spice shops — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Tel Aviv: Rothschild Boulevard & Carmel Market (Shuk HaCarmel)
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Rothschild Boulevard — Tree-lined boulevard of Bauhaus "White City" architecture. In the afternoon, continue to Carmel Market (Shuk HaCarmel) — Bustling produce, spice, and street-food market. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Caesarea Roman ruins
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Caesarea Roman ruins, a popular side trip from Tel Aviv. Return to Tel Aviv in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Caesarea Roman ruins

itinerary.title: Tel Aviv: Old Jaffa & Neve Tzedek
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Old Jaffa — Ancient port neighborhood of stone alleys, galleries, and sea views. In the afternoon, continue to Neve Tzedek — Charming, boutique-lined historic neighborhood. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Hatachana renovated railway complex — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Tel Aviv Museum of Art or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to TLV for your departure flight.

travelTips: Public transport largely shuts down for Shabbat — plan Friday evening/Saturday transport via taxi or rideshare, or stay walkable to your hotel.
Rent a Tel-O-Fun bike for an easy way to cover the boulevard, port, and beach areas.
Visit Carmel Market for lunch — the stalls and adjoining Levinsky Market are a real local food scene.
Combine a Tel Aviv beach trip with a Jerusalem day trip — they're under an hour apart.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Beachwear and sandals for the boardwalk
Light, breathable clothing for the heat and humidity
A modest cover-up for visiting religious or historic sites
thingsToDo: ANU Museum of the Jewish People
Surfing and paddleboarding off Tel Aviv beaches
Cycling the Tel Aviv coastal path
Day trip to float in the Dead Sea
thingsToAvoid: Assuming buses and trains run on Shabbat — they generally don't.
Leaving valuables unattended on the beach.
Booking travel without checking current regional advisories, given the security situation can change.
instagramSpots: Old Jaffa alleys and sea views
adventureActivities: Surfing and paddleboarding off Tel Aviv beaches
Cycling the Tel Aviv coastal path
Day trip to float in the Dead Sea
luxuryExperiences: Suite stay at a boutique Neve Tzedek hotel
Private Old Jaffa and Bauhaus architecture tour
honeymoonExperiences: Beachfront dinner on the Tel Aviv promenade
Boutique hotel stay in Neve Tzedek
budgetExperiences: Free beach days along the Mediterranean
Carmel Market wander and street food
localEtiquette: Tel Aviv is famously casual and beach-focused — dress is relaxed compared to Jerusalem.
Shabbat (Friday evening to Saturday evening) means many businesses close, though Tel Aviv stays livelier than most Israeli cities.
Tipping around 12% is standard at restaurants.
localTransportGuide: Tel Aviv is highly walkable and bike-friendly, with a citywide bike-share system (Tel-O-Fun) that's popular for getting between beach, market, and boulevard neighborhoods. Buses cover the rest of the city; note that public transit largely stops for Shabbat (Friday evening to Saturday evening), when taxis and rideshare apps are the main options.
bestTimeToVisit: April-May and September-October, for warm, comfortable weather without peak summer humidity.
weather: Mediterranean climate — hot, humid summers (highs around 30-31°C) and mild, occasionally rainy winters (highs around 18°C).
visaInformation: Visa-free entry for stays up to 90 days for US, UK, EU, Canadian, and Australian passport holders. Indian nationals need an e-visa arranged in advance. Check current entry requirements and regional travel advisories before booking, as conditions can change.
averageFlightTime: ~9.6h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Tel Aviv?
faqs.answer: Visa-free entry for stays up to 90 days for US, UK, EU, Canadian, and Australian passport holders. Indian nationals need an e-visa arranged in advance. Check current entry requirements and regional travel advisories before booking, as conditions can change.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Tel Aviv?
faqs.answer: April-May and September-October, for warm, comfortable weather without peak summer humidity. Mediterranean climate — hot, humid summers (highs around 30-31°C) and mild, occasionally rainy winters (highs around 18°C).

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Tel Aviv safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Tel Aviv is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Tel Aviv?
faqs.answer: The local currency is ILS. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Noah Rossi
reviews.title: Amazing itinerary, zero stress
reviews.body: Our trip to Tel Aviv was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Independence Hall was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Marco Patel
reviews.title: Exceeded our expectations
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Rothschild Boulevard and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Yuki Sharma
reviews.title: Worth every penny
reviews.body: Tel Aviv Museum of Art alone was worth the trip, but combined with Carmel Market (Shuk HaCarmel) this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Wei van Dijk
reviews.title: Would book again in a heartbeat
reviews.body: Booked this as a group trip and it was well paced. Independence Hall in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Sofia Ivanov
reviews.title: Solid mid-range package
reviews.body: Booked this as a solo trip and it was well paced. Carmel Market (Shuk HaCarmel) in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Marco Garcia
reviews.title: Great value for money
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Old Jaffa and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Ethan Khan
reviews.title: Exceeded our expectations
